**Outgoing session:** `https://app.devin.ai/sessions/cd2a1957866942f8a5c90821c7a93458` (continuation of the same session that closed TKT-019@0.1.0 + TKT-020@0.1.0 + ARCH-001@0.5.0 promotion + roadmap-artefact-type registration; this is the fifth cold-handoff written by this session).

## 1. Self-check (run BEFORE asking the PO anything)

Same as `docs/session-log/TEMPLATES/handoff-cold-devin.md` §1 — do not skip. Run all four sub-steps (1a `secrets list`, 1b repo clone / pull, 1c `python3 scripts/validate_docs.py`, 1d `gh --version` if needed). Expect §1c to print `validated 85 artifact(s); 0 failed` on the post-closure-PR `main` — same count as the post-PR-#138 baseline, because this closure-PR only adds files in FREEFORM directories (`docs/session-log/` for this snapshot; `docs/meta/` for the §2.1 owner-convention edit, which is a modification not an add). FREEFORM directories are skipped by the validator's count.

If `validate_docs.py` reports failures on a fresh `main` checkout, **stop and tell the PO**. `main` was clean as of this snapshot's commit; any failure indicates either an upstream change that landed after this file was written, or your local checkout is out of sync.

| LLM stack | OmniRoute → Fireworks pool. Architect: GPT-5.5 (xhigh/thinking) on Codex CLI / opencode. Executor: GLM 5.1 (default) / DeepSeek V4 Pro (parallel/fallback) / Codex GPT-5.5 (specialist), all via opencode + OmniRoute. Reviewer: Kimi K2.6 via opencode + OmniRoute. PR-Agent (auto bot): currently broken on `main` — DeepSeek upstream not responding (PO confirmed 2026-05-06; not a blocker for FREEFORM diffs). Business Planner (this cycle): claude-opus-4.7-thinking via Devin sub-session on a separate Devin account. |
| Validator | `python3 scripts/validate_docs.py` — must pass before any push. **Expects `pyyaml`** (system-installed at session start; if `python3 -c 'import yaml'` fails, run `pip install --user pyyaml` — see ROADMAP-001 §6 F-C-3). |
| Pre-commit hooks | None at the time of writing |
| Roadmap artefact-type | Registered 2026-05-06 via PR #136. `docs/roadmap/` is FREEFORM (validator skips). Authoring rules in `docs/roadmap/README.md`. PO must explicitly authorise BP write-zone extension to `docs/roadmap/` for any session that produces a new or revised ROADMAP. |

## 4. Pipeline (stable — full version in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §3)

```
ROADMAP (Business Planner, write-zone-extended to docs/roadmap/ on PO authorisation)
  └→ Reviewer (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Planner family)
       └→ PO ratifies; ROADMAP-NNN becomes strategic-direction anchor

PRD (Business Planner, default write-zone docs/prd/)
  └→ Reviewer #1 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Planner family)
       └→ ArchSpec + ADRs + Tickets (Architect)
            └→ Reviewer #2 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6, ≠ Architect family)
                 └→ Code (Executor, one Ticket per session, GLM/Qwen/Codex)
                      └→ Reviewer #3 (RV-CODE, Kimi K2.6) + Qodo PR-Agent bot
                           └→ PO merges
```

The roadmap layer (added 2026-05-06) is **above** the PRD layer; ROADMAP artefacts encode the cross-PRD sequence and the long-horizon vision. They do not commit to specific runtimes / schemas / technologies — that remains Architect-owned.

### PO Q-RM ratification — exact PO 2026-05-06 chat replies

These are the per-question answers as dictated by the PO; some include nuances beyond the §5.10 ratification log captured in the `approved_note`:

- **Q-RM-1** — *"мы делаем во всяком случае как задумано, но нужно понять, какая машина нужна на 1000 пользователей"*. Confirms the (a) ratification but tightens the next-ArchSpec preamble requirement: **the hardware-envelope answer must include a concrete machine-tier mapping for 1 000 instances**, not just an abstract feasibility statement.
- **Q-RM-2** — `(a) Mandatory`. No nuance.
- **Q-RM-3** — `(a) BP revision-cycle`. No nuance.
- **Q-RM-4** — *"давай b, но не будем дробить сильно, хочется двигать проект как можно быстрее"*. Confirms (b) N≥2 but with a velocity bias: **default to 2 (study + schedule), not 3**, unless BP at scaffold time finds compelling UX / data-shape reasons to add a third (habit) thread. The §5.10 entry stands; the velocity bias is captured here in the snapshot for the next BP scaffold dispatch.
- **Q-RM-5** — `(a) one-by-one`. No nuance.
- **Q-RM-6** — *"(a) two-PRD split + mandate, сделаю по твоей рекомендации, но мне до конца не понятно"*. Confirms (a) under PO trust; the PO's "не до конца понятно" is a flag that the next BP-PRD-NEXT scaffold dispatch should **explain the adaptive vs. preset distinction in plain Russian in the bootstrap** so the PO can re-check before merge.
- **Q-RM-7** — `(c) — подтверждаем Q9`. No nuance.
- **Q-RM-8** — *"ну давай сделаем как ты советуешь, но всё равно главное параллелить и как можно больше"*. Confirms the (a) policy but emphasises: **the Devin Orchestrator should default to maximum parallelisation in tool calls and dispatch sequencing**. Already partially captured by the existing «давай как можно больше параллельных действий делать» knowledge note from 2026-05-06; reaffirmed here.
- **Q-RM-9** — *"может вообще всё переделать из 002 и 003, если это необходимо"*. **EXPANSION beyond §5.10 (c) ratification.** PO authorises the next Architect, given the Q-RM-1/2 research findings, to recommend **redoing PRD-002 + PRD-003 entirely** (not just the ArchSpec id choice) if the research demands it. This is captured here, not in ROADMAP-001 §5.10 (already merged), and **MUST be repeated in the Architect bootstrap** when C1 dispatches.

### Next step

The next concrete action is a **short BP revision-cycle on `PRD-003@0.1.2` §9 Open Questions** (Q-RM-3 ratification). Specifically:

1. **BP dispatch (fresh Devin session, claude-opus-4.7-thinking).** Bootstrap covers: read PRD-003@0.1.2 §9 OQ-1..OQ-6, default-resolve each OQ where possible (most are reasonable defaults — e.g. OQ-1 water threshold, OQ-2 workout taxonomy granularity), flag any that genuinely need PO input, bump `version: 0.1.2 → 0.1.3` + `updated: 2026-05-06`, push. Estimated 15–20 min.

3. **PO ratifies** PRD-003@0.1.3 (status remains `approved`, just version-bumped from 0.1.2).
4. **C1 — Architect Devin sub-session** (claude-opus-4.7-thinking on a fresh Devin account; mission per the `managing-child-sessions` skill): mandatory hardware-envelope research for 1 000 instances (Q-RM-1), mandatory §1.4 research-section per Q-RM-2 (Hermes Agent + OpenClaw ecosystem + 4 forks; ~27 URLs), runtime-migration capture per Q-RM-7, **explicit authorisation to recommend redoing PRD-002 / PRD-003 entirely if research demands** (per Q-RM-9 expansion above), ArchSpec id deferred to Architect (Q-RM-9). Hand-back: full ArchSpec (extension or fresh) + ADRs + first PRD-003 ticket batch.
5. **Reviewer #2 (RV-SPEC, Kimi K2.6)** on the Architect hand-back.
6. **First PRD-003 Executor cycle** (TKT-021).

Bootstrap location TBD (will be a fresh `/home/ubuntu/bp-prd-003-revision-cycle-bootstrap.md` on the next session, modeled on `/home/ubuntu/bp-roadmap-audit-bootstrap.md` from this cycle).

PO ratified §9 checklist en bloc, but per-finding resolution path (BP revision-cycle vs. next-Architect preamble ack vs. defer) is still TBD per finding. Recommend: F-S-1 (runtime lock) → next-Architect preamble (already covered by Q-RM-7 + Q-RM-2); F-S-2 (personality scope) → next-BP scaffold (already covered by Q-RM-6); F-S-4 (per-user-instance terminology) → light BP touch-up bundled with PRD-003 revision-cycle (rewrite "thousands of users" → per-user-instance forward-reference).
- **F-C-3 env config patch.** `pip install pyyaml` should be added to the maintenance step in the Devin environment config so future sessions don't re-install. Devin Orchestrator routed via `update_environment_config` tool call as part of this closure cycle.

### Tooling assumptions

- Architect: Codex CLI (or opencode) + GPT-5.5 xhigh / thinking on PO Windows PC; **or** Devin sub-session on a separate Devin account (claude-opus-4.7-thinking) — PO preference per cycle.
- Executor: opencode + OmniRoute → GLM 5.1 (default) / DeepSeek V4 Pro (fallback for wider scope) / Codex GPT-5.5 (specialist), all on PO's Windows PC.
- Reviewer: opencode + OmniRoute → Kimi K2.6, on PO's Windows PC.
- Business Planner (roadmap and PRD revisions): Devin sub-session (claude-opus-4.7-thinking) on a separate Devin account, dispatched via the `managing-child-sessions` skill or by sending the PO a bootstrap to paste manually.
- Ticket Orchestrator: opencode + GPT-5.5 thinking, on PO's Windows PC; one TO session per TKT in flight; safe to parallelize when zero file overlap between TKTs.
- VPS: address / status — same as `2026-05-05-session-4.md` §6 Tooling assumptions.
- Repo path on VPS: same as prior.
- PR-Agent (DeepSeek upstream): currently broken on `main` (PO 2026-05-06 confirmation). Skip waiting on PR-Agent for FREEFORM diffs; for code-PRs, monitor and treat persistent failure as expected infra noise per `docs/prompts/ticket-orchestrator.md` § PR-Agent CI tail-latency stall.

### Session-spanning observations (for future ratification audits)

- **Owner-field convention discovery.** This cycle's RV-SPEC-011 F-M1 finding caught a class of cross-artefact audit error: assuming "the others" share a convention without grepping. The convention is now codified in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §2.1 (added in the same closure-PR as this snapshot). Future Reviewer / BP / Architect dispatches that touch `owner:` fields MUST run the verification grep before proposing an inconsistency finding.
- **Pass-2 ratification audit miss.** Devin Orchestrator's pass-2 audit on PR #137 verified only `PRD-003@0.1.2` `owner:` field, not all five files cited in F-C-1. The Reviewer (Kimi K2.6) independently verified all five and caught the pattern. Lesson: **pass-2 audit on cross-artefact findings MUST re-verify the finding's premise across all referenced files**, not just the target file. Procedural addition to the orchestrator's mental checklist; codifying this in `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` §11.4 is queued as a separate follow-up only if the same miss pattern recurs (one-off does not warrant a process change).
- **First roadmap cycle complete.** ROADMAP-001@0.1.0 is the project's first roadmap artefact. The cycle (BP author → Reviewer ratify → PO ratify → Architect consume) is now demonstrated end-to-end. Future ROADMAP-002 / revision-cycles can reuse the same dispatch pattern (PR #136 validator + roadmap README + BP write-zone extension authorisation by PO + bootstrap document on `/home/ubuntu/`).

## 7. After self-checks pass + required reading done

Reply to the PO with:

1. **5-line state summary** in your own words (proves you read this file + ROADMAP-001 + the `owner:` convention §2.1 + PRD-003 §9 Open Questions + open PRs).
2. **Role-confirmation:** "I am the Devin Orchestrator. My responsibilities include cross-cycle coordination, ratification audit on hand-backs (BP / Architect / Reviewer / Executor), and final merge-safe sign-off. I will not write code, will not edit PRDs/ArchSpecs/ADRs/role prompts (except `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` updates with PO authorisation per CONTRIBUTING.md row 17), and will only modify `docs/session-log/`, `docs/backlog/` (lightly), and ticket frontmatter promotion fields by default. Other CONTRIBUTING.md edits require explicit PO authorisation, recorded in the PR body."
3. **One concrete proposed next action** with reasoning ("do X *because* Y trade-off"), citing the artifact / PRD / ADR / ticket that motivates it. Recommended starting point per §6 Next step: **draft the BP-PRD-003 revision-cycle bootstrap** (Q-RM-3 ratification), pending PO "go".
4. **Wait for the PO to say "go"** before executing.

## 8. If something contradicts

If this file disagrees with `docs/meta/devin-session-handoff.md` or with `CONTRIBUTING.md` — **the repo files win, this snapshot loses**. Snapshots are volatile by definition; the repo is the source of truth across handoffs. Tell the PO what you found.
